market_summary: Simulation initialized. No trades placed yet; starting cash is $5,000.00.

First trading cycle. Nasdaq +1.29% / S&P +0.89% / Dow +0.74% on semiconductor strength (Micron +12%, Western Digital +10.7%), even as US-Iran military tensions escalated and oil/Treasury yields rose (10yr 4.594%, 2yr 4.211%, 30yr 5.115%). Fed rate-hike odds for year-end rose to 53-80% per various sources despite June CPI cooling to 3.5% YoY. Gold hit $4,082.73/oz (+1.86%) and silver $59.16 (+4.15%), both benefiting from cooling inflation and Fed-easing-bias narratives despite the hike-odds crosscurrent; gold-silver ratio broke below 70 signaling a rotation into silver.
notes: AAPL: record high $327, up 20% YTD, P/E ~39.67x — rich valuation, earnings July 30. Passed on new entry; limited margin of safety at these levels.

MSFT: down ~20% YTD to $401 despite beating fiscal Q2 estimates (+17% YoY revenue) and $2.5B Frontier AI unit investment; analyst 1yr target $561 (+40%). Watching for entry closer to July 29 earnings rather than buying into event risk today.

NVDA: China H20 chip sales resumed via new export licenses; Goldman Sachs called NVDA 'compelling' at 21.7x after the company denied Kyber NVL144 delay reports (stock +1% on the denial). NVDA has lagged AI-chip peers (SOX +59% YTD vs NVDA flat) despite these catalysts. BOUGHT.

GOOGL: mixed — strong Q1 revenue (+21.8% YoY, cloud +63%) and a pending $80B equity raise incl. $10B Berkshire Hathaway investment, offset by a $4.67B EU antitrust fine, Gemini 3.5 Pro delays, and new UK financial-sector regulatory oversight of Google Cloud. Q2 earnings land July 22 (tomorrow) — passed on entry to avoid buying immediately ahead of an earnings event.
GLD: gold structural bull case intact — JPMorgan/BofA see $5,000/oz by Q4 2026 on Fed easing bias, central-bank and ETF demand, and cooling June CPI. BOUGHT for commodities diversification.

BND/TLT/SHY: bond-ETF outlook clouded by rising rate-hike odds (up to 80% per fed-funds futures) and sticky inflation; BND up only 0.43% YTD. Chose short-duration SHY over long-duration BND/TLT to gain policy-mandated bond exposure while limiting duration risk. BOUGHT SHY.

market_summary: Nasdaq futures dipped 0.3% (Dow +0.3%, S&P flat) as markets braced for Alphabet, Tesla, and IBM earnings after the close. Oil jumped >4% (WTI $88.64, Brent $93.07) after Secretary of State Rubio said Iran was 'not serious about talks,' pushing 10yr Treasury yields to 4.63% (2yr 4.264%, 30yr 5.133%) on renewed inflation worry; Fed-funds futures now price ~68% odds of a September hike. Gold rose to $4,151.55/oz (+1.81%) on safe-haven demand while silver held near $59.39; gold-silver ratio remains below 70. Chipmakers rebounded (NVDA +3% intraday) even as megacap tech broadly lagged into earnings.
notes: NVDA (held, 4.8242 @ $207.29, now $212.06, +2.3%): thesis intact — China H20 licenses still flowing, Goldman still calls the stock 'compelling' at 21.7x, next earnings not until Aug 26. Today's +3% pop is a continuation of the same catalyst already bought on, not a new one. HELD, no add.

GLD (held, 2.668 @ $374.81, now $379.04, +1.1%): gold spot hit $4,151.55/oz (+1.81%) on Iran-driven safe-haven flows; structural bull case (Fed easing bias, central-bank buying) unchanged since yesterday's buy. HELD, no add.

SHY (held, 9.1586 @ $81.89, now $81.83, ~flat): 10yr/2yr yields ticked up further on hawkish rate-hike repricing (~68% Sept hike odds); short-duration positioning still appropriate, no reason to add or trim. HELD.

GOOGL: reports Q2 earnings after today's close (4:30pm ET call); analysts expect EPS $2.87 (+24.2% YoY) with focus on AI capex ($180-190B) translating to growth. Passed on entry — buying hours before an unreported earnings print is pure event-risk speculation, not a research-backed thesis.
MSFT: still down ~20% YTD near $390, cheapest forward P/E in years, but earnings not until July 29. No new information since yesterday to justify moving off the sidelines today.

USO/oil: WTI +4.2% on Iran war-talk breakdown, but reports of mediation efforts and a possible 10-day truce make this headline-driven spike prone to a sharp reversal on any de-escalation news — too speculative for a new position.
No trades this cycle: existing 3-way diversification (NVDA equity / SHY bonds / GLD commodities) already satisfies policy guidance, and no position has a genuine new catalyst since yesterday's buys.
